research_series.series_name: Respiration and Gas Exchange Variation Tree

measured_entities.interpretation: Useful context marker for illness,pain,metabolic load,cognitive load or exercise; not diagnostic alone.
guardrails: Do not infer disease from wearable RR alone.
Interpret by activity state and device quality.

measured_entities.interpretation: Oxygenation marker distinct from ventilation and dyspnea.
guardrails: SpO2 can be biased by skin tone,motion and sensor location.
SpO2 alone does not define tissue oxygen delivery.

measured_entities.interpretation: High-value CO2/ventilation branch when paired with symptoms,orthostatic testing or sleep data.
guardrails: Environmental CO2 is not equal to blood CO2.

measured_entities.interpretation: Supports hyperventilation/hypocapnia orthostatic branch seen in ME/CFS,long COVID/PASC contexts.
guardrails: Do not infer anxiety from hyperventilation alone.

measured_entities.interpretation: Supports sleep breathing disruption branch when combined with overnight monitoring and symptoms.
guardrails: Requires continuous overnight monitoring; not from single SpO2 spot checks.

negative_or_uncertain_findings.claim: Pulse oximetry is equally accurate across all skin tones.
SpO2/FiO2 can reliably replace PaO2/FiO2 for severity classification.
Capnography alone detects all hypoventilation events.

summary.overall_assessment: Respiration tree is usable. Strongest branch is ETCO2/hypocapnia with orthostatic symptoms; wearable SpO2/RR are useful but artifact- and context-dependent.
